**Requirements**: 8\-12 years of experience in HR leadership roles.

 **About Reverb**



Reverb is a ten year old People Operations consulting firm based in the greater Seattle area. We are a certified B Corp and woman owned business (WBENC) that supports Seattle’s startup community, women in tech, and emerging leaders.



Our team of consultants provides strategic and hands on people operations consulting to companies at every stage of growth. Every engagement is customized to meet clients where they are. We help with everything from establishing HR fundamentals (pay and performance philosophy, creating a feedback culture, inclusive hiring, and onboarding) to planning for the future.

 **Our Values**



Reverb is an equal opportunity employer committed to diversity, equity, inclusion, and belonging in the workplace. Our company is built on five core values:


* **Exceptional**
* **Flexible**
* **Kind**
* **Principled**
* **Fun Team, No Politics**

  


**The Opportunity**



We are looking for a mid\-career professional with well\-rounded People Ops (HR) experience to serve as an interim or fractional HR leader for multiple clients at once. This role blends strategic thinking, hands\-on execution, and meaningful relationship building with clients and within the Reverb community.

 **Client facing work**  

You will serve as a trusted partner to multiple clients at any given time, supporting their People Operations (HR) needs. When working with clients, you may lead the people function, coach leaders, and interact with employees at all levels. Support will range from strategic to hands\-on, and everything in between.


* Assess and deliver on client needs through conversations, data, and review of existing practices
* Proactively identify and recommend future needs and next steps to clients
* Build and execute HR priorities and roadmap tailored to each organization
* Create and implement handbooks, onboarding programs, performance cycles, and other HR systems and processes
* Provide employee relations support and practical guidance that fits the client’s culture
* Coach founders and leaders on people decisions, communication, and best practices
* Maintain clear communication, manage expectations, and build strong client relationships

  


**Internal work at Reverb**  

You will support Reverb’s business development efforts and contribute to our collaborative learning culture.


* In conjunction with staff, maintain 70 to 80 percent billable utilization annually by identifying and proposing additional needs for existing clients
* Participate in scoping conversations and assist with new client proposals
* Hold regular networking meetings and attend local community events
* Create short case studies highlighting successful client engagements
* Share tools, ideas, and best practices with fellow consultants
* Engage in team meetings, internal collaboration, and ongoing professional learning

  


**About You**



Our consultants have varied backgrounds, and here’s what makes them most successful.


* Eight to twelve years of HR experience working with teams in a wide range of industries, such as nonprofit, education, tech, gaming, or entertainment.
* Experience in a mix of mid\-size to large companies as well as startups.
* You're a flexible and proactive generalist with expertise in several aspects of HR, including performance management, employee relations, compliance, benefits, onboarding, handbook creation, and compensation.
* Excellent problem\-solving skills, including curiosity, a proactive approach, and clear communication with leaders at all levels.
* You enjoy collaboration and sharing ideas and solutions with your peers. Otherwise, you wouldn't need Reverb!
* Comfortable and confident working on and leading ambiguous projects.
* Enjoys technology; able to use tools including Google, Microsoft, Zoom, Slack, and various ATS and HRIS platforms.
* Knowledge of applicable state and federal employment laws; know the basics and when to ask for help.

  


**Reverb is an equal opportunity employer. We welcome all identities that are not rooted in harm or oppression.**

title: Founding Behavioral Health Program Director (Remote)
description:
Founding Behavioral Health Program Director

(Future VP of Behavioral Health)  
Build a Statewide Behavioral Health Division From the Ground Up\*\*  
Bridging Care — Washington State (Remote)

Bridging Care is Washington’s largest provider of community\-based care coordination under the Health Homes Program, serving 2,200\+ Medicaid members across the state. We are rapidly expanding into integrated Behavioral Health — and we’re searching for a rare clinician\-leader who can both serve clients now and build a multi\-million\-dollar statewide BH division within 12–18 months.

This is a ground\-floor, high\-impact, high\-autonomy role for someone who is both a strong clinician and a true builder.  
If you are energized by creating something meaningful, solving complex challenges, and building a Behavioral Health division that improves thousands of lives, this is a career\-defining opportunity.

This role is fully virtual and can be based anywhere in Washington State.

⭐ Your Mission

To serve clients clinically AND design, launch, and scale a statewide, virtual\-first Behavioral Health division — building the systems, workflows, team, and payer relationships needed to create a financially strong program with exceptional clinical quality.

You are not inheriting a system.  
You are building the system.

You will begin hands\-on and grow into the role of General Manager of Behavioral Health as the division becomes profitable.

⭐ What You Will Lead — AND Do

1\. Build \& Launch (Day 1\)

* Lead the creation of our WA State Behavioral Health Agency (BHA) with DOH
* Support King County BH\-ASO contracting
* Support MCO contracting (CHPW, CCW, Molina, UHC, Kaiser, Wellpoint)
* Architect workflows: referral → intake → treatment → billing → QA
* Create documentation templates and clinical standards
* Integrate BH services seamlessly with our 2,200\-member care coordination model

2\. Provide Clinical Services (Bootstrap Phase)

To keep the division cash\-light and self\-funding, you will:

* Carry a caseload of \~15–25 clients/week via telehealth
* Conduct intakes (90791\) and therapy sessions
* Maintain excellent documentation and compliance
* Begin generating revenue immediately while building the division
* This phase lasts only until the program becomes profitable — then your caseload decreases as leadership expands.

3\. Grow \& Scale (Months 4–12\)

* Hire and supervise additional clinicians (initially 1099, then W2\)
* Partner with a fractional prescriber and build an integrated therapy/medication model
* Implement dashboards, KPIs, and scalable processes
* Develop productivity, quality, and financial systems
* Build the foundation of a statewide team

4\. Operate \& Optimize (Months 6–18\)

* Own clinical quality, compliance, documentation standards, and audit readiness
* Manage toward productivity, financial performance, and strong outcomes
* Improve access, reduce no\-show rates, and build a sustainable clinical model
* Serve as the key operational leader for behavioral health
* Prepare for promotion to General Manager of Behavioral Health

⭐ Who You Are

✔ You are BOTH clinical AND operational

You enjoy providing therapy — and you love building systems, workflows, and programs.

✔ You are a builder, not a maintainer

You thrive in environments where you get to create structure, not just follow it.

✔ You think in terms of “Who, not How”

You know how to recruit the right clinicians and build a high\-performing team.

✔ You can talk to payers, regulators, and clinicians

Comfortably covering compliance, documentation, quality, outcomes, workflows, and financial performance.

✔ You are entrepreneurial and mission\-driven

You want to improve lives AND build a sustainable, profitable division.

✔ You are hungry for growth

You want a pathway to become a VP of Behavioral Health — and eventually lead a statewide scalable operation.

⭐ Qualifications

Required:

* WA license: LMHC, LMFT, LCSW/LICSW

(Must possess an active unrestricted license to practice in the state of Washington).

* Strong outpatient therapy experience
* Strong documentation \& compliance skillset
* Comfort balancing clinical work and program\-building
* Experience with telehealth
* Strong leadership instincts; desire to scale a team
* Operational thinking: processes, systems, workflows, KPIs

Preferred but not required:

* Experience with WA Medicaid, MCOs, or BH\-ASO
* Experience with DOH licensing or audits
* Experience leading teams or mentoring clinicians
* Experience in startups, integrated care, community mental health, or multidisciplinary settings

⭐ Compensation

Base Salary:

$100,000 – $135,000 (DOE, aligned with bootstrap phase)

→ Early earning potential: $120k–$150k\+

Career Path:

Clear progression to:

Director → VP of Behavioral Health  
with expanded salary, reduced caseload, and a full team under you.

Future Upside:

* Leadership development support
* Ability to build and run a multi\-million\-dollar statewide division

Benefits:

Remote work (anywhere in WA) **Medical/Dental/Vision**  PTO **Technology stipend**

⭐ Why Join Bridging Care?

You will be the founding architect of a major new service line for one of Washington’s fastest\-growing care organizations. You will have:

* A massive built\-in referral base
* Strong MCO relationships
* Full executive support
* A statewide virtual infrastructure
* True autonomy and creativity
* A real pathway to run this division as VP
* The chance to impact thousands of lives
* The opportunity to build one of WA’s most impactful BH programs

This is a rare opportunity to build AND lead — to serve AND scale — to create something life\-changing from the ground up.

Pay: $100,000\.00 \- $135,000\.00 per year

Benefits:

* Dental insurance
* Health insurance
* Life insurance
* Paid time off
* Vision insurance

Work Location: Remote

title: Corporate Counsel / General Counsel
description:
Location: Roanoke, VA



Company: 2NSystems, LLC



Employment Type: Full\-Time (Fractional/Part\-Time Considered Initially)



Reports To: CEO



Compensation: $120,000 – $150,000 \+ Bonus


***About the Role***



2NSystems is entering an exciting phase of growth—including international expansion and strategic acquisitions—and we are seeking our first in\-house legal leader to help guide us through it.



This is a rare opportunity to build and lead the legal function from the ground up while partnering directly with executive leadership on high\-impact business initiatives.



As our Corporate Counsel / General Counsel, you will serve as a strategic advisor across the organization—balancing legal risk with business objectives to help the company move quickly, confidently, and compliantly.



If you’re a business\-minded attorney who thrives in fast\-paced, high\-growth environments and enjoys wearing multiple hats, this role offers unmatched visibility and influence.


***About 2NSystems***



2NSystems is an industry leader in data center and critical facility systems, products, and turnkey services. We ensure mission\-critical facilities are properly powered, cooled, monitored, and protected.



Operating on a nationwide scale, we deliver solutions that simplify and strengthen the complete lifecycle of critical infrastructure.



Our core values—Alignment, Discipline, Respect, Partnership, Results, and Transparency—guide how we operate and grow.


***What You’ll Do***


**Strategic \& M\&A Leadership**


* Lead or support mergers \& acquisitions, including due diligence, deal structuring, negotiations, and integration
* Partner with executive leadership on growth strategy and risk management
* Identify legal risks and opportunities tied to business initiatives
* Global Expansion \& Compliance
* Advise on international expansion, including entity formation and subsidiary management
* Navigate cross\-border legal considerations (employment, data privacy, trade compliance)
* Coordinate and manage local counsel across multiple jurisdictions


**Contracts \& Corporate Governance**


* Draft, review, and negotiate commercial agreements
* Build scalable contract templates, playbooks, and approval processes
* Establish corporate governance frameworks and compliance programs
* Day\-to\-Day Legal Support
* Provide guidance on employment, IP, regulatory, and operational matters
* Manage outside counsel relationships and legal spend
* Develop internal legal processes that support a growing organization

  


***What We’re Looking For***


* J.D. from an accredited law school and active bar membership in good standing
* 5–10\+ years of legal experience, including:
* Law firm experience with a focus on corporate, M\&A, or transactional law
* Prior in\-house experience preferred (especially in high\-growth environments)
* Demonstrated experience with M\&A and cross\-border/international matters
* Strong understanding of global compliance (employment, data privacy, trade regulations)
* Excellent business judgment with a practical, solutions\-oriented approach
* Strong communication and collaboration skills across all levels of the organization
* Ability to operate independently as a standalone legal leader

  


***What Sets You Apart***



Experience supporting international expansion in multiple regions



Background working with acquisitive or private equity\-backed companies



Proven ability to build legal infrastructure in a scaling organization

 ***Benefits \& Perks***



At 2NSystems, we invest in our people and provide a competitive, well\-rounded benefits package:


* Generous PTO: 3 weeks of paid time off
* Paid Holidays: 9 company holidays \+ 1 floating holiday (your birthday!)
* Medical Coverage: Company pays 100% of the lowest\-cost plan
* Additional Medical Contributions: Toward dependent coverage
* Dental \& Vision: Company covers 50% of premiums
* 401(k) with Match: Up to 4% company match
* Life Insurance: Company\-paid at 2x salary
* Telehealth Services: Covered by the company
* Performance Bonus Opportunity
* Executive Exposure: Direct access to CEO and leadership team
* High Impact Role: Opportunity to build and shape the legal function

  


***Work Environment***



This role is based in Roanoke, VA, with flexibility considered depending on candidate experience (including hybrid or fractional arrangements initially). Some travel may be required to support acquisitions or international operations.


***Why Join 2NSystems?***



This is more than a legal role—it’s an opportunity to help shape the future of a rapidly growing company. You’ll have a seat at the table, influence major strategic decisions, and build something from the ground up in a dynamic and evolving environment.

title: Senior Accountant
description:
**Senior Accountant**

Expand your accounting knowledge and skills alongside our experienced leadership team, while managing and fostering client relationships.

**Job Title:** Senior Accountant

**Classification:** Full\-time, exempt

**Workplace Type:** Hybrid (2 days in\-office required)

**Department:** Client Services

**Reporting To:** CFO

As a **Senior Accountant** for our rapidly growing company, you are responsible for providing accounting services for the nonprofit clients that are assigned to you. You will prepare monthly financial reports, support the annual audit process, and provide other accounting services. You will also serve as the primary point of contact to answer your client’s questions. We are looking for an intrinsically motivated problem\-solver who values teamwork and impact. At The Charity CFO, you will leverage your passion for accounting and the nonprofit sector to make a lasting impact for your clients.

**Who is The Charity CFO, LLC?**

We provide premier accounting, advisory, and fractional CFO solutions for nonprofits. Our mission is to help nonprofit leaders by creating clarity and understanding of their organization’s financial position, modernizing financial operations, and bringing the focus back to what really matters \- the people and communities they serve. Our vision is to be the largest nonprofit accounting service firm in the United States.

**Our Culture \& Values**

At The Charity CFO, we put our people before revenue or clients, which has allowed us to create a unique workplace that values you first as a human being and then as an employee. We’ll provide you with all the support and training you need to crush your goals inside the workplace, along with the flexibility, benefits, and downtime to build a life you love outside the office.

Inside our doors, you’ll find a respectful, collaborative environment with team members with all levels of professional experience from beautifully diverse backgrounds. Everyone is welcome here, and you’ll be judged solely on your performance, regardless of any limitations society may have previously placed on you.

**Our Benefits**

From career advancement to free parking to 14 paid holidays, 4 weeks of PTO, and paid new parental leave, our comprehensive benefits package makes it easier to be there for your family, plan for the future and truly enjoy your downtime. Learn more about all of these benefits and more .

**What you would be helping us with:** 

* Support the team by providing training and answering technical questions.
* Regularly perform bookkeeping tasks (processing bills, recording revenue and expense transactions, posting payroll entries, etc).
* Maintain timely email correspondence with clients; answer client questions, follow\-up on outstanding requests, and coordinate regarding upcoming projects.
* Conduct month\-end close procedures for all assigned clients.
* Prepare monthly balance sheet account reconciliations (bank accounts, investments, fixed assets, prepaid expenses, lease\-related accounts, payroll accrual, deferred revenue, debt, and restricted net assets).
* Prepare monthly financial reports with an executive summary narrative for all assigned clients.
* Track spend\-down of clients’ restricted grants. Review all new client grant agreements to ensure that grants are accounted for and tracked properly and to identify any compliance expectations that may impact our accounting processes.
* Conduct a full annual review of client financial statements to ensure that they are accurate and ‘audit\-ready’ prior to the commencement of financial statement audits.
* Serve as the primary liaison with client auditors, ensuring that all requested documentation is provided in a timely manner, so that the audit timeline can remain on track.
* Prepare annual 1099 filings for all assigned clients.
* Participate in new client onboarding meetings to understand their unique needs and to build customized accounting processes.
* Participate in periodic client ‘desk reviews’ to identify areas for improvement and to develop a game plan for cleaning\-up any accounting issues.
* Maintain and rigorously document accounting processes for all assigned clients. Proactively revise accounting processes as necessary.
* Prepare budgets and forecasts.
* On an annual basis, prepare a custom assessment of each client’s financial health, leveraging our proprietary Nonprofit Blueprint model.
* Tackle client\-related projects, such as building workpapers, investigating accounting discrepancies, completing chart of account revisions, performing cash\- to accrual\-basis conversions, etc.

**Qualifications:**

* Bachelor’s degree in accounting or related field, or equivalent experience
* At least 3 years of experience in full cycle accounting or external auditing
* Energized by working with non\-profit organizations
* Have experience with Quickbooks Online or another accounting system
* Ability to work independently
* Experience with mentoring or training new team members, preferred
* Experience with nonprofit accounting, preferred
* Experience managing multiple clients, preferred

**What Success Looks Like in the First 60 Days:**

Like we mentioned above, we’ll provide you with all the support and training you need to crush your goals inside the workplace. For this role, this means that within 60 days, we will prepare you to:

* Complete the monthly close process and prepare monthly reports for assigned clients.
* Conduct a self\-review to ensure that financial statements are accurate.
* Communicate independently with clients.
* Be an ambassador for The Charity CFO, externally and internally.

**How to Apply**

We want to acknowledge that imposter syndrome is real, so bullet points aside, if our mission, culture \& values resonate with you, we want to hear from you! If you are someone who is committed to connecting the dots, supporting clients and colleagues, and want to work in a position that allows you to develop your accounting experience on work that really matters, please apply on our .

If you require special accommodations during the recruitment process, please email our recruiting team whose contact information can be found at the bottom of our .

*The Charity CFO, LLC is an equal\-opportunity employer. We do not discriminate based on race, religion, color, national origin, dietary preferences, gender, age, fashion sense, marital status, veteran status, disability status, political leanings, or any other category protected by applicable federal, state or local laws.*

**Additional Notices**

*Employment at The Charity CFO is at\-will. This job description is not an exhaustive list of all functions that may be required, and The Charity CFO reserves the right to require additional functions as well as to revise this job description at any time.*

Job Type: Full\-time

Pay: $70,000\.00 \- $85,000\.00 per year

Benefits:

* 401(k)
* 401(k) matching
* Dental insurance
* Disability insurance
* Flexible spending account
* Health insurance
* Health savings account
* Life insurance
* Paid time off
* Parental leave
* Referral program
* Retirement plan
* Vision insurance
* Work from home

Education:

* Bachelor's (Required)

Experience:

* Non\-profit accounting: 1 year (Preferred)
* Accounting software: 1 year (Required)
* full cycle accounting : 3 years (Required)

Ability to Commute:

* St. Louis, MO 63103 (Required)

Work Location: Hybrid remote in St. Louis, MO 63103

title: Independent Consultant – OCM / Project Management / Digital Adoption
description:
**Independent Consultant – OCM / Project Management / Digital Adoption (Partner Network)**

**Location:** Remote / Flexible (Client\-based engagements)  
**Engagement Type:** Independent Consultant (1099 / Fractional / Project\-Based)

**About Lampkin Brown**

Lampkin Brown is a business and technology consulting firm focused on driving enterprise transformation through **Organizational Change Management (OCM), PMO leadership, and Digital Adoption strategies**. We partner with clients across industries to deliver measurable outcomes in large\-scale transformation initiatives, system implementations, and operational improvements.

We are expanding our **partner consultant network** and are seeking experienced, high\-impact professionals to collaborate on client engagements.

**Role Overview**

We are looking for **strategic, execution\-oriented consultants** with expertise in **OCM, Project/Program Management, and Digital Adoption** to support transformation initiatives across our client portfolio.

This is not a traditional full\-time role—it is an opportunity to join a **curated network of trusted advisors** who are deployed on high\-impact engagements aligned to their expertise.

You will play a key role in helping organizations **navigate complex change, drive user adoption, and deliver business value** from transformation initiatives.

**Key Responsibilities: Organizational Change Management (OCM)**

* Develop and execute **end\-to\-end OCM strategies** aligned to business and transformation objectives
* Conduct **stakeholder analysis, change impact assessments, and readiness evaluations**
* Design and deliver **communication, engagement, and training strategies**
* Support leadership alignment and drive adoption across all levels of the organization

**Project / Program Management (PMO)**

* Establish and manage **PMO governance, reporting, and execution frameworks**
* Ensure delivery **on time, within scope, and aligned to business outcomes**
* Manage risks, dependencies, and cross\-functional coordination

**Digital Adoption \& Transformation**

* Partner with business and technology teams to improve **end\-user experience and utilization**
* Leverage tools and methodologies (e.g., **WalkMe, Whatfix, Pendo, Enablement frameworks**) where applicable
* Identify opportunities for **process optimization and continuous improvement**

**Client \& Advisory Engagement**

* Provide **thought leadership and strategic advisory** to senior stakeholders
* Contribute to **business development, proposals, and solutioning** as needed
* Represent Lampkin Brown with professionalism and a **client\-first mindset**

**Ideal Experience \& Background**

* 7–15\+ years in **OCM, Project Management, Digital Transformation, or Management Consulting**
* Proven experience supporting **large\-scale transformation initiatives** (e.g., SAP, Workday, Salesforce, custom platforms)
* Strong background in **business analysis, process improvement, and stakeholder engagement**
* Demonstrated ability to **operate independently in client\-facing environments**
* Experience working in **consulting firms (Big 4 or boutique)** is a plus

**Core Capabilities**

* Organizational Change Management (Prosci or similar methodologies preferred)
* Project / Program Management (PMP or equivalent experience preferred)
* Digital Adoption \& End\-User Enablement
* Executive communication and stakeholder management
* Analytical thinking with ability to translate insights into action

**What We’re Looking For**

* A strong **ownership mindset and accountability for outcomes**

* Opportunity to work alongside **experienced consulting leaders**
* Flexible, project\-based work aligned to your expertise
* Ability to contribute to and shape **client solutions and delivery approaches**

If you’re a seasoned consultant in **OCM, PMO, or Digital Adoption** looking to partner on meaningful transformation work, we’d love to connect.

title: Chief of Staff to the Founder (Executive & Family Office Operations)
description:
Company: Terrence Murphy Companies (encompassing Murphy Rental Holdings, Murphy Signature Homes, Synergy Sports Capital, and related portfolio companies and personal ventures)

Location: On\-site at Terrence Murphy Companies, Bryan/College Station, Texas

Reports to: Terrence Murphy, Founder \& CEO

Employment Type: Full\-time, Exempt, On\-site (no remote option)

Compensation: Competitive base salary \+ performance bonus \+ benefits (commensurate with experience; details provided during interview process)

This title reflects how top entrepreneurs, CEOs of multi\-venture real estate/development firms, and UHNW family office principals structure the role. It is commonly called Chief of Staff (CoS) in family offices, private equity\-adjacent operations, and founder\-led companies with blended personal/business demands (e.g., UHNW family offices, real estate developers, and portfolio company operators). Unlike a traditional Executive Assistant (focused primarily on logistics and admin), or a pure Chief of Staff in large corporations (who often manages teams), this role blends high\-touch strategic execution, personal optimization, and operational leverage—exactly as seen in family office CoS positions supporting principals with lifestyle, investments, developments, brand/PR, and cross\-venture coordination. It is not a people\-management role but functions as the Founder’s strategic right\-hand partner and “force multiplier.”

**Position Summary**

As Chief of Staff to the Founder, you will serve as Terrence Murphy’s trusted strategic partner and central point of coordination across all personal, professional, development, and family office activities. You will manage the Founder’s time, health, communications, and high\-priority initiatives with discretion, proactivity, and excellence. You will translate vision into flawless execution—optimizing calendars, personal wellness, vendor/banking relationships, bid/invoicing processes, Level 10 meetings, brand/PR/marketing, and communications with the fractional Family Office and wealth managers. You will anticipate needs, solve pain points, create repeatable processes, attend meetings on the Founder’s behalf when needed, and drive efficiency so the Founder can focus on high\-impact strategy, speaking engagements, and growth. This role demands exceptional judgment, confidentiality, and the ability to operate at the intersection of personal lifestyle optimization and complex business operations in real estate development, homebuilding, rentals, and portfolio ventures.

**Key Responsibilities**

1\. **Executive \& Calendar Optimization**

* Own the Founder’s full calendar (business \+ personal): schedule and prioritize all meetings, speaking engagements, travel (domestic/international), events, and commitments using best\-in\-class tools and triage systems.
* Protect high\-value focus time while ensuring seamless logistics for all activities.
* Attend Level 10 meetings and other key sessions on the Founder’s behalf when required; take comprehensive notes, distill action items, communicate follow\-ups, and track completion.

2\. **Personal \& Lifestyle Management**

* Coordinate all personal health and wellness schedules: trainers, physical therapy, massage therapy, nutritionist consultations, workouts, and any related appointments or logistics—ensuring 100% adherence and proactive conflict resolution.
* Manage personal and office\-related items, miscellaneous requests, and ad\-hoc tasks with efficiency and discretion.
* Support personal ventures and portfolio companies by facilitating communication, scheduling, and follow\-through.

3\. **Business Development, Operations \& Vendor Coordination (Murphy Rental Holdings \& Urban Development)**

* Lead and strengthen vendor relationships, banking relationships, and coordination with engineers, architects, design teams, and other partners for new development projects.
* Oversee bid processes, invoicing, scheduling, and design coordination for Murphy Signature Homes.
* Track project timelines, flag risks, and drive timely execution of deliverables.

4\. **Communication, Digital Presence \& Brand Management**

* Manage the Founder’s email inbox, LinkedIn/social media inboxes, and all inbound messages with a defined triage/response pipeline (draft responses, delegate, or escalate as appropriate).
* Oversee and optimize terrencemurphy.com and synergysportscapital.com inbound inquiries and miscellaneous requests.
* Support brand, PR, and marketing initiatives: draft communications, coordinate with external teams, and ensure consistent messaging across ventures.
* Voice the Founder’s communicative strategies to the broader team and ensure alignment.

5\. **Strategic Execution, Process Improvement \& Family Office Liaison**

* Identify and eliminate pain points; design and implement scalable processes and systems to increase efficiency in the workplace and personally.
* Optimize nutrition, health, business strategy, finance tracking, Level 10 meetings, execution, and effective communication.
* Serve as key liaison with the fractional Family Office, new wealth managers, and related advisors—facilitating back\-and\-forth communication, preparing summaries, and ensuring seamless information flow.
* Support strategic projects across all ventures, ensuring alignment with the Founder’s vision.

6\. **General**

* Maintain the highest levels of confidentiality, professionalism, and integrity at all times.
* Travel occasionally as needed to support key meetings, site visits, or events.
* Perform other duties as assigned to optimize the Founder’s time, health, and impact.

7\. **Murphy Signature Homes Operations \& Coordination**

* Lead all bid processes, manage vendors, subcontractors, independent contractors, and construction\-related activities for new projects and ongoing developments at Murphy Signature Homes.
* Serve as the primary liaison between vendors, subcontractors, independent contractors, and the company to ensure clear communication, timely invoicing, accurate scheduling, design coordination, and issue resolution.
* Oversee project timelines, construction bids, vendor performance, and execution for all Murphy Signature Homes initiatives, proactively identifying risks and driving deliverables to completion.

**Qualifications \& Ideal Candidate Profile**

* 7\+ years of progressive experience in a Chief of Staff, senior Executive Assistant, or similar strategic support role—ideally in family offices, real estate development, private equity, multi\-venture operations, or UHNW principal support.
* Proven track record supporting a high\-profile executive or entrepreneur with blended personal/business demands (calendar, health/wellness, developments, vendor management, communications).
* Exceptional organizational, project management, and problem\-solving skills; ability to juggle multiple priorities in a fast\-paced, dynamic environment.
* Strong financial and operational acumen (comfortable with banking/vendor coordination, invoicing, bids, and basic project tracking).
* Excellent written/verbal communication, emotional intelligence, and discretion.
* Tech\-savvy with proficiency in Google Workspace/Microsoft 365, calendar tools (e.g., Calendly), CRM/project management software (e.g., Asana, Notion), and basic analytics/dashboards.
* Bachelor’s degree required; MBA or relevant advanced certification preferred.
* Real estate, development, or family office experience strongly preferred.
* Must be on\-site in Bryan/College Station, Texas, and able to pass a comprehensive background check.

**Key Performance Indicators (KPIs) \& Performance Reviews**

Success will be measured objectively with clear, trackable metrics. Reviews will include self\-assessment, Founder feedback, and (where applicable) 360 input from key stakeholders.

**Monthly KPIs (Reviewed in 30\-minute check\-ins)**

* 100% on\-time and accurate calendar management with zero double\-bookings or missed high\-priority items.
* 95%\+ adherence rate for personal health/wellness schedules (workouts, PT, nutritionist, etc.).
* Email/social inbox triage: 90%\+ of messages processed within 24 hours (with defined response pipelines).
* 100% follow\-through on action items from attended meetings or delegated tasks.
* All vendor/banking communications and development deliverables tracked and escalated proactively (zero missed deadlines on assigned items).

**Quarterly KPIs (Reviewed in 60\-minute deep\-dive meetings)**

* Minimum of 3–5 documented process improvements or efficiency initiatives implemented (measured by time saved or pain points resolved).
* 100% completion rate of strategic projects/initiatives assigned (on time and within scope).
* Stakeholder satisfaction: Positive feedback (via informal surveys or Founder/team input) on vendor relationships, meeting preparedness, and communication effectiveness.
* Brand/communication support: All inbound website/social inquiries handled with documented triage and response quality.
* Measurable optimization: e.g., Founder’s high\-value time increased (tracked via calendar analysis) and personal/business goals advanced (e.g., health metrics on track, development milestones met).

**Semi\-Annual KPIs \& Performance Review (Formal 90\-minute review \+ goal setting)**

* Overall strategic impact: Alignment of all activities with Founder’s vision (assessed via goal achievement scorecard).
* Efficiency gains: Quantifiable reduction in low\-value tasks for the Founder and improved operational cadence across ventures (e.g., faster bid/invoicing cycles, stronger vendor performance).
* Long\-term optimization: Contributions to nutrition/health, brand/PR, finance/communication strategies, and Family Office coordination (measured by qualitative feedback and qualitative/quantitative outcomes).
* Professional growth: Demonstration of increasing autonomy, foresight, and leadership in execution.
* Year\-over\-year improvement in all monthly/quarterly KPIs.

Performance will be rated on a clear scale with tied incentives. Reviews emphasize proactive problem\-solving, discretion, and results that directly amplify the Founder’s capacity and impact.

**Additional Strategic Responsibilities \& Best\-Practice Details from Industry Leaders**

Drawing from Chief of Staff roles supporting top entrepreneurs, CEOs of real estate/development firms, private equity managing partners, and family office principals (as seen across leading family offices, founder\-led companies, and UHNW operations), the following expanded responsibilities and details further define success in this position. These elements are commonly integrated to maximize leverage for visionary leaders:

* Briefing \& Meeting Preparedness: Prepare detailed briefing materials, agendas, and pre\-read packets for all meetings, speaking engagements, and calls; anticipate questions and provide background context so the Founder enters every interaction fully informed and strategic.
* Strategic Planning \& Special Projects: Support annual and quarterly strategic planning; own cross\-functional special projects (e.g., new venture launches, portfolio company integrations, or efficiency audits) from scoping through execution and post\-mortem analysis.
* Anticipation \& Risk Management: Proactively anticipate Founder needs, potential bottlenecks, or risks across personal, health, development, and financial matters; surface early warnings and recommend solutions before issues arise.
* Dashboard \& KPI Tracking: Build and maintain real\-time dashboards (finance tracking, project status, health metrics, vendor performance, etc.) to provide the Founder with at\-a\-glance visibility into all key areas.
* Expense \& Reimbursement Management: Handle all personal and business expense tracking, reimbursements, invoicing approvals, and basic financial reconciliation in coordination with the fractional Family Office and wealth managers.
* Proxy Representation \& Communication: Act as the Founder’s proxy in internal and external communications when appropriate; facilitate smooth information flow between the Founder, team members, portfolio companies, vendors, and advisors while maintaining the Founder’s voice and standards.
* SOP Development \& Continuous Improvement: Document and refine standard operating procedures (SOPs) for recurring processes (calendar triage, bid workflows, health scheduling, inbox management, etc.) to create institutional knowledge and scalability.
* Brand, PR \& Marketing Amplification: Collaborate on brand storytelling, PR opportunities, content calendars, and marketing initiatives across terrencemurphy.com, synergysportscapital.com, and all ventures to elevate the Founder’s and companies’ public presence.
* Portfolio \& Family Office Integration: Deepen coordination with portfolio companies and the fractional Family Office by preparing consolidated reports, scheduling alignment meetings, and ensuring all wealth\-management communications are timely, accurate, and actionable.

This role is ideal for a high\-caliber professional who thrives on ownership, anticipates needs before they arise, and derives satisfaction from enabling extraordinary outcomes for a visionary leader. If you are ready to operate as the strategic backbone of a dynamic portfolio of companies and personal ventures, we encourage you to apply.

To apply, please submit your resume, a brief cover letter explaining why this role aligns with your experience, and two professional references. Terrence Murphy Companies is an equal opportunity employer.

SEND YOUR RESUME \& COVER LETTER TO MANAGEMENT@TERRENCEMURPHY.COM

Benefits:

* Dental insurance
* Health insurance
* Paid time off
* Vision insurance

Work Location: In person

title: Fractional Controller / Finance Lead (Accounting & Finance Group)
description:
**Job Overview:**  
We are seeking a highly experienced **Fractional Controller / Finance Lead** 1\-2 days per week to oversee accounting operations, financial reporting, and strategic finance initiatives for our US\-based operation. This role combines **hands\-on controller responsibilities with forward\-looking financial leadership**, supporting executive decision\-making and company growth.

The ideal candidate brings deep accounting expertise, experience managing **international finance operations**, and the ability to implement scalable processes across a distributed team.

This position is designed for a senior professional who can deliver **controller\-level discipline and finance leadership without a full\-time commitment** for a small, but growing company. ISL Analytics Inc. has a team of 3 full\-time employees in the US and 30 full\-time employees in the Philippines. Position is fully remote, with great flexibility in working hours!

**Key Responsibilities:**

**Financial Management \& Reporting**

* Oversee monthly, quarterly, and annual close processes across entities
* Prepare and review financial statements (P\&L, balance sheet, cash flow)
* Ensure GAAP compliance and accuracy of consolidated reporting
* Develop executive reporting packages and financial dashboards
* Analyze financial performance and provide actionable insights

**Accounting Operations \& Team Oversight**

* Oversee day\-to\-day accounting operations (AP, AR, payroll, general ledger)
* **Directly supervise and support the Accounting/Finance Manager in the Philippines office**, ensuring alignment, quality, and timeliness
* Establish and enforce internal controls and accounting policies
* Review reconciliations, journal entries, and close deliverables
* Improve close timelines and accounting workflows

**International Accounting \& Transfer Pricing**

* Oversee accounting across U.S. and international entities
* Ensure proper **intercompany accounting, eliminations, and consolidations**
* Support and maintain **transfer pricing policies and documentation**
* Ensure compliance with international tax and regulatory requirements
* Partner with external advisors on cross\-border accounting and tax matters

**Strategic Finance \& Business Partnering**

* Support budgeting, forecasting, and variance analysis
* Provide insights on financial performance, margins, and cost optimization
* Partner with leadership on pricing strategy and project profitability
* Support financial planning tied to growth, hiring, and investments
* Act as a **finance business partner** to operational and delivery teams

**Systems \& Process Improvement**

* Evaluate and optimize accounting and financial systems
* Implement scalable processes to support company growth
* Improve integration between accounting, project, and analytics systems
* Drive automation and efficiency across finance workflows

**Compliance, Audit \& Risk Management**

* Ensure compliance with tax, audit, and regulatory requirements
* Coordinate with external auditors, tax advisors, and stakeholders
* Maintain documentation for audit readiness
* Identify and mitigate financial and operational risks
* Strong organizational skills with the ability to manage multiple priorities effectively in a fast\-paced setting.

**Qualifications**

* Relevant certifications such as CPA (Certified Public Accountant) or CMA (Certified Management Accountant) are highly desirable.
* 10–15\+ years in accounting/finance, including Controller or Finance Lead experience
* Experience with **international operations and intercompany accounting** required
* Familiarity with **transfer pricing frameworks and cross\-border accounting practices** preferred
* Experience in **software, professional services, or analytics\-driven organizations** strongly preferred
* Strong knowledge of GAAP and financial reporting standards
* Experience with accounting systems (e.g., QuickBooks, NetSuite, Sage, Deltek Vision)
* Proven ability to manage remote or international teams
* Ability to operate both strategically and hands\-on
* Strong communication skills with executive stakeholders

Join us if you’re eager to lead impactful financial initiatives while working alongside a passionate team dedicated to excellence! This role offers the chance to shape financial strategies that empower a software company to succeed—apply today!

Pay: $90\.00 per hour

Work Location: Remote

title: Account/Sales Executive
description:

title: Chief Revenue & Dental Payer Partnerships Officer
description:
**Type:** Initially Part\-Time / Fractional with Equity; planned path to Full\-Time Executive Role

**About DentaSmart.ai**

DentaSmart.ai is building a simpler, smarter, and more transparent future for oral health. We help people understand their dental health, make more confident treatment decisions, and avoid surprise costs through AI\-powered oral health guidance, second\-opinion support, ongoing monitoring, and a coming dental benefits membership model. The company positions itself as **“Your AI Oral Health Guide”** and is supported by leaders across AI, dentistry, and dental benefits.

**Why This Role Matters**

Dental insurance is expensive, confusing, and often frustrating for patients, employers, and providers. DentaSmart sees a major opportunity to work with forward\-thinking dental insurers, payers, TPAs, and benefits organizations to create better member experiences, smarter navigation, more preventive engagement, and more affordable care pathways. DentaSmart’s team already includes advisors with deep dental benefits leadership experience, and this role is designed to accelerate payer partnerships into a core growth engine.

**The Opportunity**

We are looking for a senior commercial leader with **10\+ years of dental insurance industry experience** and **deep, established relationships across dental insurance companies, dental benefit organizations, TPAs, and related ecosystem partners**.

This person will help shape and lead DentaSmart’s payer strategy from the ground up. In the early stage, this will likely begin as a **part\-time or fractional executive role with meaningful equity upside**, with the expectation that it can evolve into a **full\-time Chief Revenue role** as partnerships mature and revenue scales.

This is an ideal role for someone who knows the dental benefits market deeply, understands how decisions get made inside payer organizations, and wants to help build something category\-defining.

**Key Responsibilities**

* Develop and lead DentaSmart’s **dental insurance and payer partnership strategy**
* Open doors and build senior relationships with:
* Dental insurance carriers
* Dental benefit administrators
* TPAs
* Medicare Advantage / Medicaid dental program stakeholders
* Employer benefit intermediaries and channel partners
* Identify and structure partnership models such as:
* Member engagement and education programs
* Preventive oral health navigation
* AI\-powered second\-review offerings
* Cost\-transparency and treatment\-understanding tools
* Dental benefits alternative or supplemental models
* Employer and payer co\-branded solutions
* Own the full partnership lifecycle from strategy and outreach to negotiation, pilot design, launch, and expansion
* Help define DentaSmart’s payer value proposition in terms of:
* Better member experience
* Improved prevention and early intervention
* Reduced confusion and friction
* Increased trust and transparency
* Better navigation to appropriate care
* Collaborate with product and leadership teams to shape payer\-ready offerings, workflows, and reporting
* Advise on benefit design, reimbursement logic, channel strategy, compliance considerations, and go\-to\-market sequencing
* Build a strong partnership pipeline and drive pilots into long\-term commercial agreements
* Represent DentaSmart in executive meetings, conferences, payer discussions, and industry events
* Help recruit and shape the future revenue, partnerships, and payer\-success organization as the company scales

**Required Profile**

* **10\+ years of experience** in the dental insurance, dental benefits, or payer ecosystem
* Strong existing relationships with executives and decision\-makers across dental insurers, payers, benefit administrators, and related organizations
* Proven track record of sourcing, negotiating, and closing strategic partnerships or revenue\-generating deals
* Deep understanding of:
* Dental benefit design
* Dental networks
* Claims and utilization dynamics
* Payer economics
* Employer\-sponsored dental benefits
* Member engagement and care navigation
* Experience working with commercial dental plans; experience with Medicare, Medicaid, or managed care is a plus
* Strong executive presence with the ability to speak credibly to both business and industry stakeholders
* Comfortable operating in an early\-stage startup environment where strategy and execution happen in parallel
* Able to translate market insight into concrete product, partnership, and growth recommendations

**Preferred Background**

* Former executive, VP, GM, Head of Partnerships, or senior commercial leader at a dental insurance company, dental benefits organization, TPA, or adjacent health benefits company
* Experience launching new payer products, channel partnerships, or employer\-facing dental benefit programs
* Experience in digital health, insurtech, healthcare navigation, or benefits innovation is a plus
* Familiarity with regulatory, compliance, and managed\-care considerations in dental benefits
* Strong network among consultants, brokers, benefits leaders, and dental ecosystem influencers

**Desired Traits**

* Commercially sharp and relationship\-driven
* Strategic, but highly practical
* Credible in the boardroom and effective in the field
* Entrepreneurial and comfortable building from zero
* Mission\-oriented, with real passion for fixing what is broken in dental benefits
* Able to work cross\-functionally with founders, product, clinical advisors, and future GTM leaders

**What Success Looks Like in the First 12 Months**

* Refine and validate DentaSmart’s payer partnership thesis
* Build a high\-quality pipeline of target dental insurance and benefits partners
* Secure strategic meetings with senior payer decision\-makers
* Help structure and close initial pilot, channel, or commercial partnership opportunities
* Shape a payer\-ready offering that fits real market demand
* Establish DentaSmart as a credible innovation partner in the dental benefits ecosystem
* Create a roadmap for scaling into a full\-time revenue and partnerships function

**Why Join DentaSmart**

* Opportunity to help reshape a large, outdated market
* Direct access to founders and strategic influence on company direction
* Product and category timing aligned with rising demand for transparency, prevention, and consumer\-friendly oral health tools
* Advisory alignment with leaders in dental benefits and clinical dentistry
* Significant upside as the business grows from early traction into broader partnerships and revenue scale

**Compensation**

Because DentaSmart is an early\-stage startup, we want to be transparent: this role will likely **start as a part\-time / fractional executive position with equity participation**, and is expected to transition into a **full\-time leadership role** as revenue, partnerships, and funding milestones are achieved.

We are open to structuring compensation in a way that aligns incentives and reflects the value of the relationships, experience, and commercial leadership this person brings.

**Application Process**

Please send:

* Resume or LinkedIn profile
* Brief note summarizing your relevant dental insurance and partnership experience
* Examples of payer, benefits, or channel partnerships you have led
* A few lines on how you would approach building DentaSmart’s dental insurance partnership strategy in the first 6 months

Pay: $120,000\.00 \- $150,000\.00 per year

Benefits:

* Dental insurance
* Flexible schedule
* Health insurance
* Paid time off

Work Location: Remote

title: Human Resources Business Partner
description:
NorthgateHR Partners is conducting a confidential search on behalf of a well\-established, family\-owned manufacturing organization located in Medley, Florida.

With over 45 years of operational success, this company is a recognized leader in manufacturing walk\-in coolers and freezers serving the hospitality, retail, restaurant, and supermarket industries.

This is a high\-impact role and a key addition to the company’s leadership team. The Human Resources Business Partner will work directly with ownership and senior leadership to strengthen HR infrastructure, support operational excellence, and help position the organization for continued growth.

This opportunity is ideal for a hands\-on HR professional who thrives in manufacturing environments and is motivated by the ability to influence culture, performance, and organizational effectiveness.

**Position Summary**

The Human Resources Business Partner will serve as a strategic and operational leader responsible for managing all aspects of human resources while aligning people strategies with business objectives. This individual will play a critical role in enhancing workforce performance, supporting leadership, and building scalable HR practices.

Key Responsibilities

**Strategic HR Partnership**

* Partner directly with ownership and leadership to align HR strategies with business goals
* Provide coaching and guidance to managers to strengthen leadership effectiveness and employee accountability
* Support organizational growth through the implementation of effective HR programs and initiatives

**Employee Relations \& Performance Management**

* Manage employee relations matters, including investigations, conflict resolution, and disciplinary processes
* Conduct employee evaluations and performance assessments, providing actionable feedback and coaching
* Foster a positive, accountable, and performance\-driven workplace culture

**Talent Acquisition \& Workforce Development**

* Lead the full recruitment lifecycle, including sourcing, screening, interviewing, and onboarding
* Identify workforce development needs and implement training and development initiatives
* Support employee engagement and retention strategies

**HR Operations, Payroll \& Compliance**

* Oversee weekly payroll administration with accuracy and confidentiality
* Develop and maintain HR policies, procedures, and documentation
* Ensure compliance with all applicable federal, state, and local employment laws and regulations
* Support workplace safety compliance, reporting, and related initiatives

**Systems, Reporting \& Process Improvement**

* Utilize HRIS platforms such as Paylocity to manage employee data and reporting
* Leverage technology and artificial intelligence tools to improve efficiency and streamline HR processes
* Maintain accurate HR records and ensure confidentiality

**Required Qualifications**

* Bachelor’s degree in Human Resources or related field
* Minimum of 5–7 years of progressive HR experience, including HR Generalist and/or HR Manager responsibilities
* Prior experience supporting a manufacturing, production, or industrial workforce environment is required
* Strong knowledge of employment law and HR best practices
* Experience using HRIS platforms such as Paylocity or similar systems
* Fluency in English and Spanish (spoken and written)
* Proven ability to build relationships and influence at all levels of an organization
* Demonstrated success supporting organizational growth and cultural development
* Strong problem\-solving, decision\-making, and organizational skills
* Experience leveraging technology and AI tools to enhance operational efficiency
* Ability to maintain strict confidentiality and professionalism

**Why This Opportunity Stands Out**

* Direct partnership with company ownership and executive leadership
* Opportunity to shape and strengthen HR infrastructure
* High\-visibility leadership role with meaningful organizational impact
* Established, stable company with long\-term growth trajectory
* Ability to influence culture, performance, and operational effectiveness

**About NorthgateHR Partners**

NorthgateHR Partners is a strategic human resources consulting firm providing fractional HR leadership and workforce solutions to growing organizations. We partner with companies to build strong people operations that support business growth, performance, and long\-term success.

This search is being conducted confidentially on behalf of our client.

Pay: From $85,000\.00 per year

Benefits:

* Paid time off

Application Question(s):

* Do you have prior HR experience in a manufacturing, production, or industrial environment?
* This is a stand\-alone HR role. Are you comfortable managing both strategic HR initiatives and day to day HR operations such as employee relations, payroll oversight, and recruiting?

Experience:

* Human resources management: 5 years (Required)

Language:

* English and Spanish (Required)

Ability to Commute:

* Medley, FL 33166 (Required)

Work Location: In person

title: Fractional Controller - Remote
description:
Position Description: The Controller is responsible for the overall integrity, timeliness, and usefulness of the companys financial information and related systems. This primarily remote role (with occasional travel to the restaurants and support office) oversees accounting operations, implements and improves financial processes, and provides actionable analysis and key performance indicators (KPIs) to support executive and operations management. The Controller will serve as a key business partner to ownership and leadership, helping to drive profitable, well\-controlled growth as the company expands its restaurant base.  

  

Additional Information:
Qualifications  

* 510 years of progressive accounting/finance experience in a multi\-unit restaurant environment (corporate or large franchise group).
* Demonstrated experience overseeing or heavily using an ERP system tailored to restaurant or multi\-unit retail businesses (e.g., Restaurant365, similar platforms).
* Experience adding new stores or entities into an ERP environment, including configuration, testing, and go\-live support.
* Proven track record of designing, implementing, and improving processes around daily sales recording, deposit and cash reconciliation, and POS\-to\-GL integration.
* Prior responsibility for preparing or managing store\-level and consolidated KPIs and dashboard reporting for executive and operations teams.
* Experience managing financial operations across multiple states, including multi\-state sales tax, payroll tax, and tip reporting compliance.
* Hands\-on experience with new restaurant /unit opening and expansion, including financial support, pre\-opening budgets, build\-out cost tracking, and initial P\&L setup.
* Technical Skills
* Strong proficiency with restaurant ERP systems; specific knowledge of Restaurant365 is a plus.
* High proficiency with Microsoft Excel and comfort working with data extracts, pivot tables, and lookups.
* Familiarity with POS systems, bank portals, and payroll platforms commonly used in the restaurant industry.
* Experience with business intelligence or data visualization tools (e.g., Power BI, Tableau, or built\-in ERP reporting) to create dashboards and ad hoc analyses.
* Familiarity with third\-party delivery platform portals and reporting (e.g., DoorDash, Uber Eats, Grubhub) and their financial reconciliation requirements.
* Working knowledge of lease accounting standards (ASC 842\) and multi\-state sales and payroll tax requirements.
* Competencies and Attributes
* Strong analytical and problem\-solving skills, with the ability to translate data into clear, action\-oriented insights for non\-financial leaders.
* Exceptional attention to detail and a commitment to accuracy and integrity in all financial reporting.
* Demonstrated ability to operate effectively in a primarily remote environment while maintaining strong relationships with on\-site restaurant and support teams.
* Excellent communication and interpersonal skills, with the ability to collaborate across departments and interact confidently with owners, executives, external accountants, brokers, and lenders.
* Hands\-on, proactive, and process\-improvement mindset, comfortable working in a growing, multi\-unit family\-owned restaurant business.
* Ability to manage competing deadlines across multiple locations and entities while maintaining accuracy during high\-volume close periods.
* Working understanding of restaurant unit economics, including prime cost analysis, four\-wall profitability, and labor productivity metrics.

Work Environment and Travel
* This role is part\-time, non\-exempt.
* Weekly hour expectation will be 20\-25\.
* This role is eligible to be fully remote; however, candidates residing in Buffalo, NY or Phoenix, AZ are preferred


Rate: 85,000\.00 \- 95,000\.00 DOE


Periodic travel to restaurants and the support office may be required to maintain relationships, understand operations firsthand, assist with openings of new locations, and support implementation and monitoring of financial systems and controls.

  


Job Advertisement:
Key Responsibilities


1\. Financial Reporting and Analysis  

* Oversee the month\-end and year\-end close process, ensuring accurate and timely financial statements for all entities and locations.
* Prepare and analyze consolidated and store\-level P\&Ls, balance sheets, and cash flow information, highlighting trends, variances, and opportunities for improvement.
* Develop and maintain dashboards and regular KPIs that are meaningful to executive and operations management (e.g., sales by daypart, labor and food cost metrics, prime cost, check average, guest count, waste and void trends).
* Provide ad hoc financial and operational analysis to support pricing decisions, promotions, new product introductions, and capital investments.

  


2\. Budgeting, Forecasting, and Performance Management
* Lead the annual budgeting process, including store\-level and consolidated budgets for sales, labor, food and paper, controllables, and capital expenditures.
* Develop rolling forecasts and cash flow projections to support growth plans and capital allocation.
* Partner with operations leadership to set targets, track performance versus budget, and drive accountability for results at each restaurant.

  


3\. Restaurant ERP, Systems, and Daily Cash/Sales Processing  

* Serve as the primary owner of the restaurant enterprise resource planning (ERP) environment, including general ledger, AP, AR, fixed assets, and operational modules.
* Add new restaurants and related entities to the ERP, ensuring proper configuration of chart of accounts, store/location structure, tax settings, and integrations with POS and banking platforms.
* Design, implement, and continuously improve systems and controls around daily sales reporting, deposits, and cash management, including reconciliation of POS sales to bank deposits and ERP.
* Work to streamline and automate the flow of information between POS, Restaurant365 (or other ERP), payroll providers, and banking systems to reduce manual work and error risk.
* Manage reconciliation of third\-party delivery platform revenue (e.g., DoorDash, Uber Eats, Grubhub), including commission tracking, payment verification, and proper revenue recognition across locations.
* Oversee accounting for gift card programs, including liability tracking, breakage recognition, and escheatment compliance across applicable jurisdictions.

4\. Cash Management, Treasury, and Working Capital  

* Oversee daily cash positioning, cash flow forecasting, and banking relationships.
* Monitor and manage AR, AP, and inventory levels to optimize working capital while supporting operational needs.
* Ensure appropriate controls over cash handling at each restaurant, including safe counts, over/short procedures, and deposit verification.

5\. Internal Controls, Compliance, and Tax
* Maintain and improve internal control systems across the restaurant network, including segregation of duties, approval hierarchies, and documented procedures.
* Coordinate the preparation of sales tax, payroll tax, and other regulatory filings, working with external accountants as appropriate.
* Ensure compliance with applicable federal, state, and local regulations affecting the restaurant business.
* Monitor and ensure compliance with multi\-state sales tax obligations, including varying food and beverage tax rates and exemptions.

6\. Insurance, Risk Management, and External Relationships  

* Maintain proper insurance coverage on all company assets, including property, general liability, auto, workers compensation, and other appropriate policies.
* Work with insurance brokers to evaluate coverage levels, manage renewals, and coordinate claims.
* Serve as the primary point of contact for external accountants, providing schedules, analyses, and support for year\-end reviewed/audited financial statements and tax filings.
* Support and manage relationships with bankers and other lenders, including covenant compliance, information requests, and periodic review meetings.
* Serve as primary point of contact for landlords and ensure timely payments of rent, and follow through of any landlord requests.

7\. Inventory, Cost Control, and Profitability  

* Oversee inventory and costing processes, including periodic counts, cost updates, and margin analysis by item and location.
* Partner with operations to monitor and improve food, paper, labor, and controllable expenses, using store\-level metrics and KPIs to identify issues.
* Support menu engineering and supplier negotiations with accurate cost and margin data.
* Track and analyze third\-party delivery commissions, packaging costs, and net profitability by delivery channel to inform partnership decisions.
* Monitor waste, spoilage, and shrinkage trends by location, implementing controls and reporting to reduce loss.

8\. Leadership and Collaboration  

* Lead and develop a small, high\-performing accounting and finance team, including internal and external resources as appropriate.
* Collaborate closely with operations, HR, and executive leadership to align finance processes with restaurant needs.
* Champion a culture of accountability, continuous improvement, and service to internal stakeholders.

9\. Lease Administration and Occupancy Costs  

* Manage lease accounting and ensure compliance with ASC 842 for all restaurant and office locations, including right\-of\-use asset and lease liability calculations.
* Track and analyze occupancy costs (rent, CAM, property taxes, utilities) by location to support site\-level profitability analysis and lease renewal decisions.
* Provide financial modeling and analysis for new location openings, including build\-out cost tracking, pre\-opening expense budgets, and break\-even projections.


Qualifications

* Education and Credentials
* Bachelors degree in accounting, finance, or a related field required.
* CPA designation is preferred but not required.

title: Fractional Controller - Remote
description:
Position Description: The Controller is responsible for the overall integrity, timeliness, and usefulness of the companys financial information and related systems. This primarily remote role (with occasional travel to the restaurants and support office) oversees accounting operations, implements and improves financial processes, and provides actionable analysis and key performance indicators (KPIs) to support executive and operations management. The Controller will serve as a key business partner to ownership and leadership, helping to drive profitable, well\-controlled growth as the company expands its restaurant base.  

  

Additional Information:
Qualifications  

* 510 years of progressive accounting/finance experience in a multi\-unit restaurant environment (corporate or large franchise group).
* Demonstrated experience overseeing or heavily using an ERP system tailored to restaurant or multi\-unit retail businesses (e.g., Restaurant365, similar platforms).
* Experience adding new stores or entities into an ERP environment, including configuration, testing, and go\-live support.
* Proven track record of designing, implementing, and improving processes around daily sales recording, deposit and cash reconciliation, and POS\-to\-GL integration.
* Prior responsibility for preparing or managing store\-level and consolidated KPIs and dashboard reporting for executive and operations teams.
* Experience managing financial operations across multiple states, including multi\-state sales tax, payroll tax, and tip reporting compliance.
* Hands\-on experience with new restaurant /unit opening and expansion, including financial support, pre\-opening budgets, build\-out cost tracking, and initial P\&L setup.
* Technical Skills
* Strong proficiency with restaurant ERP systems; specific knowledge of Restaurant365 is a plus.
* High proficiency with Microsoft Excel and comfort working with data extracts, pivot tables, and lookups.
* Familiarity with POS systems, bank portals, and payroll platforms commonly used in the restaurant industry.
* Experience with business intelligence or data visualization tools (e.g., Power BI, Tableau, or built\-in ERP reporting) to create dashboards and ad hoc analyses.
* Familiarity with third\-party delivery platform portals and reporting (e.g., DoorDash, Uber Eats, Grubhub) and their financial reconciliation requirements.
* Working knowledge of lease accounting standards (ASC 842\) and multi\-state sales and payroll tax requirements.
* Competencies and Attributes
* Strong analytical and problem\-solving skills, with the ability to translate data into clear, action\-oriented insights for non\-financial leaders.
* Exceptional attention to detail and a commitment to accuracy and integrity in all financial reporting.
* Demonstrated ability to operate effectively in a primarily remote environment while maintaining strong relationships with on\-site restaurant and support teams.
* Excellent communication and interpersonal skills, with the ability to collaborate across departments and interact confidently with owners, executives, external accountants, brokers, and lenders.
* Hands\-on, proactive, and process\-improvement mindset, comfortable working in a growing, multi\-unit family\-owned restaurant business.
* Ability to manage competing deadlines across multiple locations and entities while maintaining accuracy during high\-volume close periods.
* Working understanding of restaurant unit economics, including prime cost analysis, four\-wall profitability, and labor productivity metrics.

Work Environment and Travel
* This role is part\-time, non\-exempt.
* Weekly hour expectation will be 20\-25\.
* This role is eligible to be fully remote; however, candidates residing in Buffalo, NY or Phoenix, AZ are preferred


Rate: 85,000\.00 \- 95,000\.00 DOE


Periodic travel to restaurants and the support office may be required to maintain relationships, understand operations firsthand, assist with openings of new locations, and support implementation and monitoring of financial systems and controls.
